description: Out-of-band human-approval gate for AgentFlow. OpenApprovalTool escalates high-impact actions over SMS; ResolveApprovalTool executes the captured payload when the approver's decision turn arrives. State lives in an Autonomous DB table via ORDS; nothing blocks, no compute is pinned.

tools.description: Escalate an action to a human approver over SMS. Writes a pending row via ORDS, texts the approver, returns an approval_id. The agent's next reply to the requester should reference this ID.

schema.name: action_summary
schema.description: Plain-language description of what will happen if approved. Include the amount, destination, and reason. Shown verbatim to the approver over SMS.

schema.name: action_payload
schema.description: The concrete action to execute on approval. Opaque to this tool. On approve, either POSTed to conf.execute_webhook_url or returned as queued_action for the caller flow to dispatch.

schema.name: requester_ref
schema.description: Session key / phone number to text the outcome back to. Verified from the inbound relay, not user-typed.

schema.name: approver_allow
schema.description: Allowed approver phone numbers. List of E.164 strings (e.g. ['+15551234567', '+15559876543']). Also accepted as comma-separated string or JSON-encoded list.

schema.name: conversation_ref
schema.description: Optional audit reference (thread ID, chat URL). Stored in the row; never gated on.

credential_name.help: AIDP Credential Store display name OR OCI Vault secret OCID. Bundle keys: ords_username, ords_password, twilio_account_sid, twilio_auth_token, twilio_from_number, and optionally ords_base_url.

ords_base_url.help: ORDS module base URL, e.g. https://<host>/ords/aidp/hitl. Overridden by credential bundle's ords_base_url if present.

ttl_hours.help: Time after which the row is swept to 'expired'. Default 48h. The sweep is a DBMS_SCHEDULER job, not this tool.

execute_webhook_url.help: Optional. Where to POST the action_payload on approve. If empty, the payload is returned as queued_action for the caller flow to dispatch.

tools.description: Called by the HITL agent when the approver's decision turn arrives. ORDS runs an atomic authorize + compare-and-set; on ok+approve the captured action_payload is executed. The requester is SMSed the outcome.

schema.name: approval_id
schema.description: The 6-character approval ID the approver typed. Case-insensitive; the tool uppercases before lookup.

schema.name: decision
schema.description: 'approve' or 'reject'. Any string starting with 'a' becomes approved; 'r' becomes rejected.

schema.name: sender_ref
schema.description: Approver's VERIFIED phone number from the inbound relay. Do not accept a user-typed value here — this is the identity check the whole gate depends on.
